title: Thank God Its Doomsday

jokes.text: I scream, you scream, we all scream for... Haircuts!
jokes.setup: Classic ice cream jingle sets up expectation for 'ice cream'
jokes.punchline: Subverts to 'haircuts' for kids' barbershop ad
jokes.explanation: Clean misdirection with good economy. Works as commercial parody but not exceptional.

jokes.text: But we'd like our hair to look like... people hair.
jokes.setup: Kids want to switch from Marge's home haircuts
jokes.punchline: Implies Marge's cuts make them look inhuman
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment that's both funny and believable. Great economy with 'people hair.'

jokes.text: But they don't have my templates.
jokes.setup: Marge's resistance to kids getting professional haircuts
jokes.punchline: She uses templates for home haircuts like a factory
jokes.explanation: Good character detail about Marge's systematic approach to everything, including bad haircuts.

jokes.text: They're baking fresh Cinnabons... which means they're throwing out the old ones!
jokes.setup: Homer offers to drive kids to mall
jokes.punchline: His real motivation is discarded pastries
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - helping family is secondary to food opportunity.

jokes.text: Which side do you want your part on? Left! Right! Stab! Stab! Stab! You're the boss.
jokes.setup: Professional barber asking simple styling question
jokes.punchline: Escalates to violent stabbing motions with scissors
jokes.explanation: Simple escalation gag. The violence is cartoonish but predictable.

jokes.text: Bart, you look worse than Aunt Patty in the morning. And there ain't nothin' looks worse than Aunt Patty in the morning.
jokes.setup: Homer comments on Bart's bad haircut
jokes.punchline: Doubles down on insulting Aunt Patty for emphasis
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer meanness delivered with conviction. The double-down structure works well.

jokes.text: My bad-boy spikes! My good-girl points!
jokes.setup: Kids getting their hair ruined at the 'professional' barbershop
jokes.punchline: Their signature hairstyles are destroyed
jokes.explanation: Nice callback to their established character designs. Shows consequences of their choice.

jokes.text: while I return some socks that appeared to be black, but were in reality a very dark blue
jokes.setup: Skinner explaining his mall errand to students
jokes.punchline: Overly precise description of trivial sock color difference
jokes.explanation: Perfect Skinner pedantry. The specificity makes it funny and character-specific.

jokes.text: The best photographs, or 'photos', will be prominently displayed
jokes.setup: Skinner giving photography assignment instructions
jokes.punchline: Unnecessarily defines 'photographs' as 'photos'
jokes.explanation: More Skinner over-explanation. Good character consistency.

jokes.text: I love you, Dumpster-bons.
jokes.setup: Homer eating discarded Cinnabons from dumpster
jokes.punchline: Affectionate portmanteau of 'dumpster' and 'Cinnabons'
jokes.explanation: Excellent wordplay that's both clever and perfectly in character. High quotability.

jokes.text: Big surprise. The fat guy's eating garbage.
jokes.setup: Kids discover Homer eating from dumpster
jokes.punchline: Bart's deadpan reaction treats it as predictable
jokes.explanation: Good family dynamic - Bart's blasé attitude toward Homer's behavior.

jokes.text: The Principal's got a shirt wiener!
jokes.setup: Bart looking for interesting photo subjects
jokes.punchline: Discovers Skinner's shirt creates phallic silhouette
jokes.explanation: Typical Bart juvenile humor. The visual gag works but is fairly predictable.

jokes.text: Leave your body, Seymour. Leave your body.
jokes.setup: Skinner realizes he's being photographed in embarrassing position
jokes.punchline: Attempts meditation to escape the mortification
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment with Skinner's characteristically over-intellectual response to embarrassment.

jokes.text: I'd rather play golf on the holiest day of the week.
jokes.setup: Marge wants Homer to come to church
jokes.punchline: Homer calls Sunday the holiest day while refusing church
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer hypocrisy - acknowledges sanctity while rejecting observance.

jokes.text: Finally, a character I can relate to. I bet good things happen to him.
jokes.setup: Homer identifying with character in rapture movie
jokes.punchline: Dramatic irony - audience knows bad things will happen
jokes.explanation: Good setup for dramatic irony. Homer's optimism contrasts with impending doom.

jokes.text: Science has shown religion is just an old wives' tale.
jokes.setup: Character dismissing religious concerns
jokes.punchline: Classic setup for immediate divine retribution in disaster movie
jokes.explanation: Standard disaster movie trope setup. Predictable but functional.

jokes.text: Where did my Christian limo driver go?
jokes.setup: Rapture beginning, people disappearing
jokes.punchline: Oddly specific concern about driver's religion
jokes.explanation: Good specificity in the chaos. The detail makes it funny.

jokes.text: My pious husband is missing! The baby I chose to have baptized is gone!
jokes.setup: Woman discovering family taken in Rapture
jokes.punchline: Emphasis on 'chose to have baptized' implies calculation
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about parental choices. The specificity adds comedy.

jokes.text: I thought all religions were a path to God. I was wrong!
jokes.setup: Character's liberal religious views being challenged
jokes.punchline: Immediate conversion under pressure
jokes.explanation: Standard religious panic humor. Functional but predictable.

jokes.text: Why did I choose to be gay?
jokes.setup: Various characters regretting life choices
jokes.punchline: Treating sexual orientation as choice to regret
jokes.explanation: Problematic humor that relies on outdated stereotypes. Doesn't age well.

jokes.text: This movie will haunt me for the rest of my life, just like Cannonball Run II.
jokes.setup: Homer affected by religious movie
jokes.punchline: Compares to notoriously bad sequel
jokes.explanation: Great pop culture reference that works on multiple levels. Compares trauma to bad movie experience.

jokes.text: He'd send us signs, like, I don't know, all the dogs getting on a spaceship and leaving.
jokes.setup: Marge reassuring Homer about rapture signs
jokes.punchline: Hilariously specific and absurd example
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist humor. The specificity and visual make it memorable and quotable.

jokes.text: Yeah, I'll bet the cats would be psyched about that.
jokes.setup: Marge's scenario about dogs leaving on spaceship
jokes.punchline: Homer imagines cats' reaction to dog-free world
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up that extends the absurd scenario logically. Nice callback structure.

jokes.text: Now how about a little 'rapture' for Mama?
jokes.setup: Homer feeling reassured about the rapture
jokes.punchline: Sexual innuendo using 'rapture' as euphemism
jokes.explanation: Typical Homer inappropriate timing. The wordplay works but relies on innuendo.

jokes.text: I could be the Rachel to your Jacob. Okay, but it's tough for me not to think about their hardship.
jokes.setup: Marge making biblical romantic reference
jokes.punchline: Homer's literal interpretation ruins the mood
jokes.explanation: Good character dynamic. Marge tries romance, Homer overthinks the biblical story.

jokes.text: Blood is raining from the sky!
jokes.setup: Apparent biblical apocalyptic sign
jokes.punchline: Likely mundane explanation (red liquid from above)
jokes.explanation: Sets up apparent miracle that's probably explained by context. Standard apocalypse parody.

jokes.text: This ain't personal. I just love to scrimshaw.
jokes.setup: Captain trying to harpoon whale (implied from context)
jokes.punchline: Motivation is artistic hobby, not survival
jokes.explanation: Great character moment. Reduces epic whaling to craft hobby with perfect economy.

jokes.text: And would you like to take advantage of our 'Friends of Flanders' discount? No, thank you.
jokes.setup: Christian bookstore offering Ned Flanders discount
jokes.punchline: Homer reflexively rejects anything Flanders-related
jokes.explanation: Good callback to established character dynamics. Homer's pettiness overrides financial sense.

jokes.text: So, you still think I'm crazy for saving all your hair trimmings? No, ma'am.
jokes.setup: Marge's weird habit of saving cut hair
jokes.punchline: Kids now grateful for disturbing behavior
jokes.explanation: Good character moment revealing Marge's hoarding tendencies. Disturbing but funny.

jokes.text: Let's get the rest of this hair back in the freezer.
jokes.setup: Marge has been saving hair trimmings
jokes.punchline: Reveals she freezes hair like food
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation that makes the hair-saving even more disturbing and specific.

jokes.text: Word books!
jokes.setup: Marge noting Homer is reading books
jokes.punchline: Homer's childish description of books
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer-ism. Reduces books to most basic description with childlike wonder.

jokes.text: The Book of Revelations has 404 verses... Add the number of people at the Last Supper... minus the number of Filipinos in the Bible...
jokes.setup: Homer doing complex mathematical calculation
jokes.punchline: Includes completely arbitrary and nonsensical variable
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist math. The random specificity of 'Filipinos' makes it hilarious and quotable.

jokes.text: A week? That's... Seven days from now!
jokes.setup: Homer realizing timeline is one week
jokes.punchline: Redundantly explains what a week means
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer unnecessary clarification. Shows his mental processing.

jokes.text: The world will end next week! Spend your children's college fund! Thaw that turkey now!
jokes.setup: Homer announcing end of world
jokes.punchline: Practical advice gets increasingly mundane
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation from financial to domestic. 'Thaw that turkey' is perfectly mundane and quotable.

jokes.text: Homer, what turned you from sad drunk to mad monk?
jokes.setup: News interview about Homer's transformation
jokes.punchline: Alliterative description contrasting old and new Homer
jokes.explanation: Excellent alliterative phrase that perfectly captures Homer's transformation. Very quotable.

jokes.text: It's the end of the world! God loves you! He's gonna kill you!
jokes.setup: Homer's religious awakening
jokes.punchline: Contradictory messages about God's love and violence
jokes.explanation: Perfect contradiction captures religious paradox. Excellent economy and impact.

jokes.text: Any other cuckoo yip-yap?
jokes.setup: Kent wrapping up interview with Homer
jokes.punchline: Professional dismissal using folksy language
jokes.explanation: Good character moment for Kent's condescending professionalism. 'Yip-yap' is memorable.

jokes.text: Just before the Rapture, 'the stars will fall to the Earth.' So all you hippies out there might want to... for that one.
jokes.setup: Homer quoting biblical prophecy about falling stars
jokes.punchline: Warns hippies about astrological implications
jokes.explanation: Decent connection between biblical prophecy and hippie culture. Not exceptional.

jokes.text: Even a few cabbages are getting in on the fun.
jokes.setup: Kent's enthusiastic lettuce festival coverage
jokes.punchline: Anthropomorphizes cabbages as party crashers
jokes.explanation: Nice escalation that treats vegetables like social climbers. Good absurdist touch.

jokes.text: Marge, in a world gone mad, only a lunatic is truly insane.
jokes.setup: Marge commenting on Homer's crazy exercise routine
jokes.punchline: Homer's pseudo-philosophical justification
jokes.explanation: Good pseudo-philosophical Homer moment. The circular logic is characteristically nonsensical.

jokes.text: A hundred years, global warming-- we're goners, but for now, do you think you could lighten up on this 'left below' stuff?
jokes.setup: Lisa acknowledging long-term environmental concerns
jokes.punchline: Requests relief from immediate doomsday talk
jokes.explanation: Good Lisa moment balancing her environmental awareness with family dynamics.

jokes.text: I thought we were getting Charlie's Angels.
jokes.setup: Blue Angels flyover at event
jokes.punchline: Krusty expected different, more entertaining Angels
jokes.explanation: Simple misdirection joke playing on similar names. Decent but not inspired.

jokes.text: I don't wanna die next to Kathy Griffin!
jokes.setup: Celebrity afraid of dying in plane crash
jokes.punchline: More afraid of dying near specific comedian
jokes.explanation: Celebrity roast humor that's mean-spirited but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: To all the fans of Los Lobos, mucho, mucho condolences.
jokes.setup: Krusty addressing celebrity crash aftermath
jokes.punchline: Casual condolences for band's fans with Spanish flourish
jokes.explanation: Good dark humor about celebrity death. The 'mucho mucho' adds character touch.

jokes.text: I've done stuff I ain't proud of, and the stuff I am proud of is disgusting.
jokes.setup: Moe asking Homer to save his soul
jokes.punchline: Admits both shameful and proudly disgusting behavior
jokes.explanation: Excellent Moe line that perfectly captures his character. The contradiction structure is brilliant and quotable.

jokes.text: Lie naked on our roof? Cause that girl's school says I can't do that no more.
jokes.setup: Barney asking about rapture preparation
jokes.punchline: Reveals inappropriate past behavior with school
jokes.explanation: Classic Barney inappropriate behavior reveal. Character-appropriate but relies on creep factor.

jokes.text: No, Flanders. It's... a meeting of gay witches for abortion. You wouldn't be interested.
jokes.setup: Flanders discovering Homer's rapture gathering
jokes.punchline: Homer lists everything Flanders would hate
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer strategy - combines all of Flanders' religious fears. Great character understanding.

jokes.text: Chief Wiggum, wanna be my buddy? What's wrong with Carl?
jokes.setup: Homer asking Wiggum to be rapture buddy
jokes.punchline: Wiggum immediately asks about Homer's usual friend
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Wiggum's awareness of friendship dynamics.

jokes.text: Trouble in paradise?
jokes.setup: Wiggum noting Homer and Carl's separation
jokes.punchline: Treats male friendship like romantic relationship
jokes.explanation: Standard bromance humor. Wiggum's delivery likely sells it better than the line itself.

jokes.text: But sweetheart, I have something they didn't have-- a good feeling about this.
jokes.setup: Lisa noting all failed prophecies throughout history
jokes.punchline: Homer's only advantage is baseless confidence
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic. Reduces complex theological history to personal intuition.

jokes.text: 99 minutes until we're all saved 99 minutes to go Unless it turns out that you're not devout Then you're gonna be left down below
jokes.setup: Countdown song parodying '99 Bottles'
jokes.punchline: Religious anxiety set to familiar drinking song
jokes.explanation: Decent parody that works structurally. The irony of using drinking song is appropriate.

jokes.text: I'm gonna steal God's secrets and sell 'em to Satan!
jokes.setup: Everyone expressing gratitude to Homer
jokes.punchline: Moe reveals corrupt plan for heaven
jokes.explanation: Perfect Moe line showing his criminal mindset even in religious context. Highly quotable and character-specific.

jokes.text: Lisa, you still believe in me, don't you? Dad, if you'll recall, I never believed in you, not for one second.
jokes.setup: Homer seeking family support after failure
jokes.punchline: Lisa's brutal honesty about never believing
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lisa moment. Her intellectual honesty delivered with deadpan precision.

jokes.text: That's my girl.
jokes.setup: Lisa completely destroying Homer's confidence
jokes.punchline: Homer's proud reaction to being insulted
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment showing Homer's pride in Lisa's intelligence even when it hurts him.

jokes.text: Hey, Nostra-dumbass, did the Rapture come? I can't recall. In fact I can recall, and it didn't, and you suck.
jokes.setup: Nelson mocking Homer's failed prediction
jokes.punchline: Escalates from fake memory loss to direct insult
jokes.explanation: Good Nelson bullying with clever structure. 'Nostra-dumbass' is a decent portmanteau.

jokes.text: You said the end was comin', so I sold the bar to some Japanese businessmen and gave the money to charity. Now them orphans got new skip ropes, and I end every day smelling like eel.
jokes.setup: Moe explaining what happened to his bar
jokes.punchline: Good deed resulted in personally unpleasant consequences
jokes.explanation: Great Moe story with perfect character detail about unintended consequences. The eel smell is specific and funny.

jokes.text: Cats are all over me.
jokes.setup: Moe smelling like eel
jokes.punchline: Logical consequence creates new problem
jokes.explanation: Nice logical extension of the eel smell. Creates vivid mental image.

jokes.text: 12 Apostles! But Jesus was at the last supper, too! This changes everything.
jokes.setup: Homer recalculating his rapture math
jokes.punchline: Realizes he miscounted last supper attendees
jokes.explanation: Good callback to earlier math with logical error discovery. Shows Homer's process.

jokes.text: No, make sandwiches! And some Kettle Chips would be nice! Original, not barbecue!
jokes.setup: Homer panicking about new rapture timing
jokes.punchline: Apocalypse preparations become snack preferences
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation showing Homer's priorities. The chip specificity is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: You couldn't predict 6:00 at 5:30!
jokes.setup: Marge dismissing Homer's prediction abilities
jokes.punchline: Reduces prophecy to basic time-telling
jokes.explanation: Brilliant putdown with perfect economy. Reduces cosmic prophecy to mundane inability.

jokes.text: I went to Lisa's school play, which had serious pacing problems.
jokes.setup: Homer complaining family won't attend his rapture
jokes.punchline: Petty criticism of Lisa's play as comparison
jokes.explanation: Good Homer petty complaint. The theatrical criticism is unexpectedly specific and funny.

jokes.text: So beautiful with your many rings.
jokes.setup: Homer viewing Earth from heaven
jokes.punchline: Mistakes Earth for Saturn with rings
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer geographical confusion. Shows his scientific ignorance even in heaven.

jokes.text: Look, just don't use leprechaun labor. Okay? Don't do it.
jokes.setup: God explaining heaven's water slide delay
jokes.punchline: Warns against specific problematic workforce
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist detail about heavenly construction problems. Very quotable and unexpected.

jokes.text: Okay, just for that, your room's next to the kiddie pool, all right, Mr. Smarty-Head Exploder?
jokes.setup: Homer making head explode wish
jokes.punchline: God's petty punishment and name-calling
jokes.explanation: Great characterization of petty God. The punishment fits the crime with childish logic.

jokes.text: My Son went down to Earth once. I don't know what you people did to Him, but He hasn't been the same since.
jokes.setup: Homer asking God about family suffering
jokes.punchline: God's parental concern about Jesus's traumatic Earth experience
jokes.explanation: Brilliant reframing of crucifixion as parental trauma. Dark, original, and deeply quotable.

jokes.text: Buddy, You just made Yourself a powerful enemy.
jokes.setup: God refusing to help Homer's family
jokes.punchline: Homer threatens the Almighty
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer hubris. The casual threat to God is both absurd and in character.

jokes.text: Screw you, Dino! You squandered your gift!
jokes.setup: Homer angry in heaven
jokes.punchline: Takes out frustration on Dean Martin
jokes.explanation: Good random target for Homer's anger. The celebrity criticism is appropriately petty.

jokes.text: I made 68 albums.
jokes.setup: Homer accusing Dino of squandering talent
jokes.punchline: Dean's defensive response with specific number
jokes.explanation: Decent defensive response. The specific number adds authenticity to the celebrity portrayal.

jokes.text: Superman did it.
jokes.setup: God saying turning back time is difficult
jokes.punchline: Homer cites comic book precedent
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic applying pop culture to theology. Great economy and character consistency.

jokes.text: You want me to help you with your alcoholism? No, I'm in a good place with that.
jokes.setup: God offering to help Homer
jokes.punchline: Homer content with his drinking problem
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer self-awareness and acceptance. The casual dismissal of divine intervention is hilarious.

jokes.text: It was all just a dream. What are these wings? It's just a seagull stuck in my back.
jokes.setup: Standard 'it was all a dream' ending
jokes.punchline: Dream explanation immediately undermined by absurd reality
jokes.explanation: Clever subversion of dream ending trope. The seagull explanation is perfectly absurd.

jokes.text: This is heaven. Hallelujah!
jokes.setup: Homer back with family
jokes.punchline: Treats mundane family life as paradise
jokes.explanation: Nice ending callback showing Homer's newfound appreciation. Completes the character arc.

tmdb_overview: Homer sees a movie about the end of the world and fears the same thing will happen in real life after seeing a chain of random occurrences and doing a complicated math equation that predicts the end will come on May 18th.
